title: gh#58: R1.3 — Implement LinkedInPublisher and FacebookPublisher
reasoning:
Imported from https://github.com/joshgk00/social-media-scheduler/issues/58.
Backlog section: Candidate 1 — Publisher seam.

## Context

Third PR in Candidate 1. Mirrors R1.2 for the other two platforms. After this PR, all three concrete Publishers exist; the worker switches over in R1.4.

## Scope

- Add `packages/worker/src/publishers/linkedin.ts`:
  - `createLinkedInPublisher(): Publisher`
  - Owns the 3-step image upload sequence (`initializeUpload` → `PUT` → `/rest/posts`), aborting before the `/rest/posts` call if any earlier step fails so no orphaned image URN is referenced
  - Folds `classifyLinkedInError` inside as a private function
  - Reads `visibility` and `media[0]` (LinkedIn = single image) from `PublishablePost`
- Add `packages/worker/src/publishers/facebook.ts`:
  - `createFacebookPublisher(): Publisher`
  - Owns the video path (`POST /{pageId}/videos`) and multi-photo carousel path (N × `/{pageId}/photos` then `/{pageId}/feed`)
  - Note: partial-failure orphaned photo IDs (the existing `FacebookPublishApiError.orphanedPhotoIds`) are **dropped** at the Publisher boundary — they were set-but-never-read (verified during grilling). If a future caller needs them, surface as a follow-up issue
  - Folds `classifyFacebookError` inside as a private function
  - Reads `linkUrl` and `media[]` (Facebook = video OR multi-photo) from `PublishablePost`
- Token-shape redaction inside each Publisher; same discipline as R1.2
- Unit tests for each: success, permanent failure, transient failure, redaction

## Out of scope

- Wiring changes to `publish-worker.ts` (R1.4)
- Removing the old platform services (R1.5)
- Surfacing orphaned photo IDs to a higher layer — explicit non-goal per ADR-0002 staging

## Acceptance

- `pnpm --filter @sms/worker build` passes
- `pnpm --filter @sms/worker test` passes
- Test fake fixtures available for both: `createFakeLinkedInPublisher`, `createFakeFacebookPublisher`

## References

- ADRs: [0001](../blob/main/docs/adr/0001-publish-failure-is-thrown-not-returned.md), [0002](../blob/main/docs/adr/0002-publisher-receives-raw-profile-row.md)
- Depends on: #56

history.reasoning: Original evidence paths still do not exist: packages/worker/src/publishers/linkedin.ts and packages/worker/src/publishers/facebook.ts both returned missing, and packages/worker/src/publishers contains only twitter.ts. Current code has service-level implementations in linkedin-publish.service.ts and facebook-publish.service.ts, but rg found no createLinkedInPublisher, createFacebookPublisher, createFakeLinkedInPublisher, or createFakeFacebookPublisher anywhere. publish-worker.ts still wires callLinkedIn/callFacebook services and imports classifyLinkedInError/classifyFacebookError from @sms/shared/lib/error-classifier, so the requested Publisher boundary has not been implemented. Existing tests cover callLinkedIn/callFacebook services and the Twitter publisher only; there are no LinkedIn/Facebook publisher tests or fake fixtures. pnpm --filter @sms/worker typecheck passes. Build and targeted Vitest runs could not complete because the read-only sandbox blocked writes to dist and node_modules/.vite-temp, so those failures are environmental rather than evidence the issue is fixed.
commands: pwd && rg --files packages/worker/src packages/worker/test packages/worker/tests 2>/dev/null | sort

history.reasoning: The original evidence paths now exist in the current working tree: packages/worker/src/publishers/linkedin.ts and packages/worker/src/publishers/facebook.ts. LinkedIn implements createLinkedInPublisher, private classifyLinkedInError, token redaction, visibility/media[0] handling, the initializeUpload -> PUT -> /rest/posts image flow, and aborts before /posts when PUT fails. Facebook implements createFacebookPublisher, private classifyFacebookError, token redaction, video /videos publishing, multi-photo /photos then /feed publishing, linkUrl/media[] handling, and drops orphanedPhotoIds at the PublishFailure boundary. Both fake fixtures exist. Focused publisher tests exist for success, permanent failure, transient failure, redaction, LinkedIn abort, Facebook orphan-boundary behavior, and fake fixtures. pnpm --filter @sms/worker typecheck passed, and a separate tsc --noEmit check over the new publisher tests passed. Vitest/build could not fully run in this read-only sandbox because Vite/tsc attempted to write .vite-temp/dist files, but runtime smoke checks with encrypted credentials and mocked fetch confirmed the key LinkedIn and Facebook flows and redaction/orphan-boundary behavior. The worker still imports shared classifiers and old services in publish-worker.ts, but wiring changes were explicitly out of scope for this finding.
commands: pnpm --filter @sms/worker exec vitest run src/__tests__/publishers/linkedin.test.ts src/__tests__/publishers/facebook.test.ts --configLoader runner --no-file-parallelism

pnpm --filter @sms/worker exec tsc --noEmit --target ES2022 --module NodeNext --moduleResolution NodeNext --strict --esModuleInterop --skipLibCheck --types vitest/globals src/__tests__/publishers/linkedin.test.ts src/__tests__/publishers/facebook.test.ts
